Kiran Mazumdar-Shaw, builder of India's leading biotech enterprise and a pioneer in the development of biologics and biosimilars, shares her story and learnings from business

Podcast Episode # Kiran Mazumdar-Shaw, builder of India's leading biotech enterprise and a pioneer in the development of biologics and biosimilars, shares her story and learnings from business Biotech CEO Sisterhood

Video •

Sep 2, 2025

48 min 11 sec

## Episode Description

Interviewed by Sisterhood editorial board member Sheila Gujrathi, Kiran Mazumdar-Shaw describes her beginnings in designing enzymes, and eventually pioneering biologics and biosimilars after an initial success in making recombinant human insulin for the India market. She discusses her emphasis on affordable innovation and shares business learnings from her career.

See all episodes

## More episodes like this

Show all

* * *

### Legal Innovation at Scale: How CAM Is Becoming an AI-First Law Firm

Video • CAM Radio: A Cyril Amarchand Mangaldas Podcast

In this episode, Our Chief Innovation Officer, Komal Gupta in conversation with Partner, Richa Roy explore legal innovation at scale in CAM, one of India’s largest law firms, transforming how it imagines and delivers legal services.From the early adoption of AI-powered due diligence tools to firmwide ideation programs like Vichaar, the launch of India’s first legal tech incubator Prarambh, and the rollout of generative AI platforms such as Legora and Copilot, this conversation traces CAM’s multi-year journey from experimentation to becoming an AI-first organisation.The discussion goes beyond technology to examine how innovation truly works inside a law firm, the cultural shifts required, the importance of design thinking and client-centric problem solving, the role of policy and risk management, and why empowerment and transformation sit at the heart of sustainable change.Key themes include the difference between legal and non-legal AI tools, responsible AI adoption, data confidentiality and governance, building internal capability, driving adoption across offices, and preparing lawyers for a future where legal excellence and digital fluency are equally essential.A must-watch for lawyers, in-house counsel, policymakers, and anyone interested in how AI and innovation are reshaping the legal profession in India and beyond.

Dec 19, 2025

49 min 16 sec

* * *

### Enabling Future Ready Youth and Communities Everywhere - A Conversation with Karishma Galani

Future Learning Design Podcast

This is the second part episode in this mini-series looking into the ways in which artificial intelligence is impacting the lives of teachers and young people around the world, through the lens of recently announced partnerships with Anthropic. In this conversation, I explored with Karishma Galani the way that Pratham Education Foundation, one of the largest NGOs in India, is integrating AI capabilities and platforms into its work reaching millions of underserved young people with quality education. They announced a partnership with Anthropic just over a month ago, but that is growing from much deeper roots in the vision of Pratham’s co-founder, Madhav Chavan, that you will hear Karishma talk about.Karishma is the Co-Lead of PraDigi Innovation Centre at Pratham International. She leads a lot of the digital innovation and AI work at Pratham, building on her long career in tech startups, research & development and venture capital. Karishma founded a deeptech company developing educational assessment powered by machine learning out of Singapore and London and she has been an active researcher at the MIT Media Lab. Karishma is also an author of two books, 'Maker Minds' and 'Making A Shift: Social Entrepreneurship in Schools'https://www.anthropic.com/news/bengaluru-office-partnerships-across-india

Mar 21

27 min 20 sec

* * *

### Pankhuri Sharma, Strategy and Operations Leader at Humana

Becker’s Payer Issues Podcast

In this episode, Pankhuri Sharma, Strategy and Operations Leader at Humana, joins the podcast to discuss gaps between population health strategy and real-world outcomes. She shares key priorities for effective population health programs and offers practical advice for emerging leaders navigating the evolving healthcare landscape.

Apr 9

10 min 25 sec

* * *

### Where AI Meets the Road | Ambica Rajagopal, Michelin | Live at IndiaAI Impact Summit

Video • The CAIO Connect Podcast (Chief AI Officer Connect Podcast)

Recorded live at the India AI Summit, this episode of CAIO Connect features Ambika Rajgopalan, Chief AI & Data Officer at Michelin.In this candid conversation, Ambika breaks down what it truly takes to scale AI inside a global enterprise — from data foundations and governance to culture, ROI, and execution discipline. She shares insights on embedding AI into business strategy, navigating transformation at scale, and leading responsibly in an era of accelerated innovation. This is a masterclass in enterprise AI leadership — grounded, practical, and future-focused.

Feb 26

4 min 33 sec
